Description:
MSTDN+ is a new English-language, general-purpose instance open to everyone! If you appreciate my work, please consider contributing via Open Collective.

I've been a Mastodon instance admin and user since 2019, and I opened this instance to make sure there are more reliable general-purpose instances available for people to choose from during the great #TwitterMigration :)

Note: NSFW content is prohibited on this instance. Alternatively you may use mstdn.party.

Abuse / Harassment: If you are being harassed, please report the user using the "…" button and then "Report." Moderators may take any action they deem appropriate, such as a warning or an expulsion.

When reporting accounts, please make sure to include at least a few posts that show rule-breaking behavior, when applicable. If there is any additional context that might help make a decision, please also include it in the comment.

This server follows the Mastodon Server Covenant:

  1. We agree to active moderation against racism, sexism, homophobia and transphobia
  2. We agree to daily backups to ensure your data is safe
  3. We have backup administrators ready to operate this server in emergencies or my unexpected absence
  4. In the unlikely event this instance shuts down, we agree to give our users a 3 month period to migrate to another instance

As a general-purpose instance, we do not have heavy moderation in terms of what topics people are allowed to post about, however all users are expected to follow our rules at all times, and generally be nice and friendly on the federation.

Please report all content you see which might violate our rules for evaluation. If you are on a remote server, please forward any reports of our users to our server for our moderators to take action, we pledge that remote reports will remain confidential within our moderation team and will not be used for any form of retribution against the reporter.

Legal

MSTDN+ is operated by Fediverse Communications LLC, a Minnesota (USA) company, and is hosted on servers operated in Germany and Finland. All content on this server is expected to be legal in all of these jurisdictions. If you have any copyright, trademark, legal concerns, please email us directly.

  • DMCA Complaints: dmca@fediverse.us
  • Trademark Complaints: trademark@fediverse.us
  • Abuse Complaints: abuse@fediverse.us
Short: MSTDN+ is an English-speaking general-purpose Mastodon instance, welcome!

Description:
Laburo Social es una red social de laburo, no-gubernamental dedicada a ayudar trabajadores independientes y artesanos a promocionar su laburo, el objetivo es fomentar el trabajo honesto y mejorar los ingresos de quienes realmente lo necesitan, por lo que es gratis y no se cobra comisiones, sin publicidad, sin rastreadores, sin el control de las grandes corporaciones big-tech y de código abierto.

Las cuentas deben representar exclusivamente trabajadores independientes, artesanos o microempresas/locales de menos de 5 empleados. Tratar de no utilizar nombres propios en el @Tag, este campo no se puede cambiar después.

Además del nombre del negocio, indicá (1) el sector al que pertenece el servicio o producto, Ej: agricultura, arte, comida, construcción, diseño, educación, tecnología, transporte, etc y (2) de qué se trata el negocio en términos generales Ej: "peluquería unisex", "cantante de cumbia", "diseño web", "clases de crochet", "reparación de radiadores", etc

Recordá leer los términos de servicio y que la única forma de ingreso son las donaciones directas, así que si lográs conseguir guitas usando la plataforma, apoyá.

Donaciones: Cafecito/Mercado Pago
Short: Red social de trabajadores independientes, artesanos y locales pequeños de Argentina

Be warned! this Server may be unstable at times. Statistics about our uptime are available here.

Our Mozilla-Observatory scan can be found here and our Google CSP evaluator scan can be found here

This instance has no specific theme and will not block any other instance unless they spam or use bots. All blocked instances are listed publicly. Note: Users can still choose to block an instance on their own account.

This instance uses S3 storage to host content(media attachments). User content is not stored locally. As an end-user, you should not notice this.

If you want to join please write how you found this instance and why you want to join.

Wir behalten uns vor die Anmeldungen zu begrenzen sollte der Aufwand, diese Instanz zu betreiben, unsere Kapazitäten übersteigen.

Es finden regelmäßig Wartungsarbeiten an dieser Instanz und auf dem Server, auf dem diese läuft, statt. Weitere Informationen zu Wartungsarbeiten findet Ihr unter "Regelmäßige Wartung" auf fachschaften.org.

Description:
Welcome!

Welcome to Mastodon.me.uk, a social media community hosted in the UK; part of the Fediverse/ActivityPub/Mastodon federated social network, which allows you to follow users on other communities. It's a bit like Twitter but without a single company in control.

If you're new to the Fediverse, the FediTips beginners' guide is a great introduction.

Arriving from Twitter?

If you're coming from Twitter, here are a few useful tools:

  • Find people you know over there by connecting your accounts up to Fedifinder or Twitodon.
  • Many thing you might consider "missing features" are intentional; for instance, quoting other posts is intentionally not possible. Try to get comfy and learn a bit about the space before suggesting it should change.

Fediverse Etiquette (Fediquette?)

  • Use content warnings on posts that people might prefer not to see (politics most often, but you'll see many other examples). It's easy and polite.
  • Don't crosspost indiscriminately between networks.
  • #PutHashtagsInCamelCase, it makes them easier to read for everyone.
  • Add descriptions to your images. Object-Action-Context is a great method.

How We Moderate Content

Your moderation team is @Floppy, @CatherineFlick, @Darkedge, @pauljrobinson, and @jezhiggins. We hope to move to a democractically-elected mod team at some point when we work out how.

The moderation team on this instance are responsible for enforcing our Code of Conduct (see below). In general, our approach is to let people say what they like (unless clearly illegal), but prevent them saying it publicly where it can hurt others. We think that's best aligned with the principles of the Fediverse as a whole.

  1. People that clearly breach UK law (or who are considered spammers by the mods) will be removed immediately.
  2. People that attack other people or groups based on who they are (not for what they believe) will be suspended - that basically means they'll be silenced, and fully deleted after 30 days.
  3. People that post offensive content, or who post regularly without using an appropriate content warning will be limited, making their posts only visible to their followers.
  4. People that have previously posted offensive content on other networks (including Twitter) may be pre-emptively moderated here, though in general we will limit rather than suspend until the accounts actually breach the guidelines on this network.
  6. Servers that fail to meet similar standards of moderation will be limited, though we much prefer to limit individuals if we can.
  7. Servers that encourage offensive content will be blocked.

These principles apply to both local users and federated content, though obviously we can only take the ultimate action to delete content for local users.

We will react to reports as quickly as we can, though please remember we are humans and cannot always respond straight away (especially at night). And of course, we can only respond if we know about infringements, so please use the report button!

Funding

This is a volunteer-run community administered by @Floppy, and hosted by Mythic Beasts in the UK at Canary Wharf. Please support our running costs by joining the Open Collective and becoming a backer. Thanks go to our existing backers - this place exists because of them!

Service Status

Service status is available from our status page, and service updates will normally be posted as server announcements which you will see at the top of your home timeline.

Code of Conduct

Our Pledge

In the interest of fostering an open and welcoming environment, we as contributors and maintainers pledge to making participation in our project and our community a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, body size, disability, ethnicity, gender identity and expression, level of experience, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, class, or sexual identity and orientation.

Our Standards

Examples of behavior that contributes to creating a positive environment include:

  • Using welcoming and inclusive language
  • Being respectful of differing viewpoints and experiences
  • Gracefully accepting constructive criticism
  • Focusing on what is best for the community
  • Showing empathy towards other community members

Examples of unacceptable behavior by participants include:

  • The use of sexualized language or imagery and unwelcome sexual attention or advances
  • Trolling, insulting/derogatory comments, and personal attacks
  • Public or private harassment
  • Publishing others' private information, such as a physical or electronic address, without explicit permission
  • Other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional setting

Our Responsibilities

Server admins are responsible for clarifying the standards of acceptable behavior and are expected to take appropriate and fair corrective action in response to any instances of unacceptable behavior.

Server admins have the right and responsibility to remove, edit, or reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, and other contributions that are not aligned to this Code of Conduct, or to ban temporarily or permanently any contributor for other behaviors that they deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, or harmful.

Scope

This Code of Conduct applies both within project spaces and in public spaces when an individual is representing the project or its community. Examples of representing a project or community include using an official project e-mail address, posting via an official social media account, or acting as an appointed representative at an online or offline event. Representation of a project may be further defined and clarified by project maintainers.

Enforcement

Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be reported by contacting the administrator at @floppy. All complaints will be reviewed and investigated and will result in a response that is deemed necessary and appropriate to the circumstances. The project team is obligated to maintain confidentiality with regard to the reporter of an incident. Further details of specific enforcement policies may be posted separately.

Server maintainers who do not follow or enforce the Code of Conduct in good faith may face temporary or permanent repercussions as determined by other members of the project's leadership.

Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the Contributor Covenant, version 1.4, available at http://contributor-covenant.org/version/1/4.

Colophon

Description:
gender.systems is an invite-only, general-purpose instance catering to queer & left-leaning entities. Much of its core membership come from a traumaqueer/writing community known as Empty Spaces and the surrounding spheres.

Registration

Registration to gender.systems is on an invite-only basis. In order to join, you'll need to talk to someone here. Any member of gender.systems is capable of inviting someone.

Code of Conduct

Short Version

Moderation reports are handled as quickly as possible when they come in, though this may take up to 48 hours - moderation staff are all volunteers and have very busy lives.

This instance is not a free speech zone, and occurrences of fascism, transphobia, and any other hateful speech or ideology will be dealt with accordingly.

This instance is meant to be a safe and cozy space for all who inhabit it and are willing to abide by its rules. Please use content warnings when appropriate!

Long Version

This is not a legally binding document, and final interpretation is wholly up to the moderation and administration of gender.systems. They are presented to you solely to provide an insight to our moderation policies.

  1. Hate speech and discrimination are absolutely not tolerated. This is included, but not limited to: queerphobia, racism, ableism, sexism, xenophobia
  2. This is a left-wing instance and, accordingly, "centrism" and right-wing politics will not be tolerated. This includes "both-sides"-ing issues such as transphobia.
  3. Harassment of users both on gender.systems and on other instances is not allowed. This includes stalking, impeding use of the service or degrading the performance of the service. Examples include:
    • Continuing to engage in conversation with a user that has specifically requested for said engagement to end, regardless of platform-specific privacy tools.
    • Aggregating, posting, or sharing a user's personally-identifiable information (PII), known as doxing.
    • Inciting users to engage in said behavior may also be considered harassment.
  4. This instance is hosted in the United States of America, and is therefore subject to its laws. Please do not post content illegal in the USA.
  5. This is not a sexual or explicit instance, but due to the nature of its content and the users within, all members must be 18 years or older. This is an internal rule only and individual gender.systems users are not restricted from interacting with minors or vice-versa.

These rules notwithstanding, moderation reserves the right to revoke any user's access to the service at any time, for any reason, except where required by law.

Content Warning / Sensitive Media Policy

Content warnings are intended to make the timeline and the instance as a whole more comfortable for the community and those it federates with. Many members of gender.systems write about uncomfortable topics, and these may prove difficult for some users.

Mastodon provides a very helpful content warning field that can hide posts behind a line of text, providing a useful subject line for triggering content such as sexually explicit content, politics, etc.

Images can similarly be marked as sensitive, and we specifically ask that any sexually explicit content is marked as such.

Additionally, as gender.systems runs the Glitch fork and has an expanded character limit, users are requested to add content warnings to long posts as vanilla Mastodon does not have the functionality to collapse long posts.

We cannot enforce content warnings from other instances, and do not condone tone policing in any way. If you want to ask someone repost something with a content warning for any reason, please be polite and respectful.

Staff

  • @kvuzet (Owner, Maintainer)
  • @oathboundFamiliar (Admin)
  • @humvadev (Admin)
  • @retrotimelady (Moderator)

Restricted Instances

We maintain a blocklist of instances that we do not federate with. If you believe that an instance should be added to this list, please contact an admin or moderator.

For privacy and safety of our community, we do not comment on any specific instance blocks that we have in place.

The list is visible for logged-in members under the section Moderated servers below.

Attributions

Instance branding art by @oathboundFamiliar.

This instance uses Mutant Standard emoji, which are licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International License.
Short: gender.systems is an invite-only queer instance for those inhabiting the liminal spaces between worlds. Speak softly and tread carefully - the dolls are sleeping.

Description: Strings: the ActivityPub instance of Jerome Gravel-Niquet
